subject: [PATCH 1/4] mm: Move demotion related functions in memory-tiers.c

reviews.author: Joshua Hahn
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Joshua Hahn suggested moving a helpful comment from vmscan.c to memory-tiers.c, noting that it clarifies the behavior of folios in @demote_folios when not demoted.

Reviewer noted that mt_demote_folios() function is not properly protected by locks and may lead to data corruption or other issues, but the rest of the patch looks good.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Alexandre,

I hope you are doing well! Thank you for the patch.

Makes sense to move the migration functions together. Just one small
nit, I think the following comment is pretty helpful in understanding
that folios that aren't demoted still remain in @demote_folios. Should
we also move this comment to memory-tiers.c?

[...snip...]

---

[...snip...]

Anyways, the rest looks good to me. Have a great day!
Joshua

2026-03-11.patch_summary: This patch moves functions related to demoting memory usage in the Linux kernel from mm/vmscan.c to mm/memory-tiers.c, as per Alexandre Ghiti's suggestion. The goal is to centralize these functions in a single file for better organization and maintainability. No functional changes are intended by this move.
